title: Simultaneous Measurement and Correlation of PPG Signals Taken from Two Different Body Parts for Enhanced Biometric Security via Two-level Authentication
venue: Proceedings of the First ACM Workshop on the Internet of Safe Things (SafeThings’17)

method.training_strategy: Fixed threshold based on average of minimum same-subject correlation and maximum different-subject correlation

evaluation_protocol: Leave-one-out style validation using a threshold derived from a subset of 7 subjects (D1) and applied to the remaining 8 subjects in a full 15x15 cross-correlation matrix.

reported_limitations: Spurious peaks from lighting and motion artifacts

usability.usability_issues: Requires the user to keep still and use a physical pulse oximeter

key_contribution: Proposed a two-level authentication system using Pearson correlation of heart signals from two different body parts (face and finger) to prevent spoofing of physical biometrics
main_strength: Uses involuntary physiological signals which are harder to mimic than fingerprints or facial appearance
main_weakness: Lacks experimental testing against actual spoofing attacks
High dependence on controlled lighting and lack of motion
insights.notes_for_review: The paper claims 100% success, but does not provide standard metrics (EER/FAR/FRR). The a-priori thresholding on a small subset may lead to optimistic results.
